summary: A potential US-Iran peace deal is reportedly imminent, with Axios reporting an agreement could be announced within 24 hours, though key disputes remain over the wording of several provisions. Hezbollah continues daily attacks on northern Israel, including at least 15 explosive drones launched at IDF forces in southern Lebanon over the past weekend, killing one Israeli soldier and wounding two others. Iran's IRGC issued a defiant statement on the 44th anniversary of Khorramshahr's liberation, declaring that any renewed enemy aggression would receive a devastating hellish response across regional and extra-regional dimensions.
key_points: Axios reports US and Iran are close to a deal to end the war, with an agreement possibly announced within 24 hours, though disputes remain over key wording. (Source: @kianmeli1/87611)

At least 15 explosive drones were launched at IDF forces in southern Lebanon and the northern border over the past weekend, triggering 20 red alerts in confrontation-line communities. (Source: @inon_yttach/8231)

IDF soldier Noam Hamburger, 23, from Atlit, a technology and maintenance fighter in Battalion 9 of the 401st Brigade, was killed by a Hezbollah explosive drone strike in northern Israel. (Source: @almogboker78/17095)

The proposed deal includes three phases: formal end of war, resolution of the Strait of Hormuz crisis, and opening of 30-day negotiations on a broader agreement with possible extension. (Source: @amitsegal/61308)
Iran's Fars news agency reports that under the draft MOU, the Strait of Hormuz would remain under Iranian management despite Trump's claim of free passage. (Source: @pkpoi/142152)

IRGC commander-in-chief issued a statement declaring Iran's armed forces at highest readiness across missile, air, naval, ground, space, and cyber domains, warning any renewed aggression would receive a devastating response. (Source: @SharghDaily/161697)

Iranian intelligence dismantled a network of 15 suspects tied to two organized cells and a newly emerged terrorist group in Fars province before they could threaten public security. (Source: @presstv/191170)
Two IDF soldiers were wounded (moderate and light) by an explosive drone in southern Lebanon and evacuated to hospital; their families have been notified. (Source: @N12chat/208784)

sections.executive_summary: The most operationally significant development is the reported imminent US-Iran peace deal, with Axios indicating an agreement could be announced within 24 hours, potentially ending the 60-day war. However, Hezbollah continues daily attacks on northern Israel with explosive drones, killing one IDF soldier and wounding others, while Iran's IRGC has issued a defiant statement threatening devastating retaliation against any renewed aggression. The contradiction between diplomatic progress and ongoing combat operations creates a highly volatile operational environment.

points: Iran is reportedly close to a comprehensive agreement with the United States to end the 60-day war, with Axios reporting a deal could be announced within 24 hours; remaining disputes center on the wording of several key provisions. (Source: @kianmeli1/87611)

Iran's Fars news agency, affiliated with the IRGC, claims that under the draft memorandum of understanding, the Strait of Hormuz would remain under Iranian management, contradicting Trump's public statements about free passage through the strait. (Source: @pkpoi/142152)

The IRGC commander-in-chief issued a major strategic statement on the 44th anniversary of Khorramshahr's liberation, declaring Iran's armed forces at the highest level of readiness and active deterrence across all domains — missile, air, naval, ground, space, and cyber. (Source: @SharghDaily/161697)

The IRGC statement explicitly warned that any renewed enemy aggression would receive a 'devastating and hellish response across regional and extra-regional dimensions,' signaling Iran's intent to escalate beyond the immediate theater if attacked again. (Source: @SharghDaily/161697)

Iranian intelligence services announced the dismantling of a trained network of rioters and terrorist elements backed by foreign groups in Fars province, arresting 15 suspects connected to two organized cells and a newly emerged terrorist group. (Source: @presstv/191170)

Iranian state media reports that airports in Isfahan, Ardabil, and Lamard will resume operations on Sunday (3 Khordad), with no new restrictions currently applied to the country's airspace. (Source: @kianmeli1/87614)

FDD director Mark Dubowitz warned that if Trump agrees to a 60-day ceasefire based on Iran's vague promises to 'discuss' nuclear issues, it would push the crisis to late July or early August, effectively eliminating military leverage before the midterm elections. (Source: @kianmeli1/87605)

IDF Spokesperson reported hostile aircraft intrusion alerts in the Shtula and Metulla areas along the northern confrontation line, with details under investigation; the incidents at Kfar Giladi/Tzofar and Shtula/Tzofar were later declared ended. (Source: @idf_telegram/23807)

IDF forces in the Gaza Strip conducted strikes overnight to destroy structures in multiple areas, resulting in powerful explosions heard across the south and the Shephelah region. (Source: @pkpoi/142153)

IDF soldier Noam Hamburger, 23, from Atlit, a technology and maintenance fighter in Battalion 9 of the 401st 'Iron Tracks' Brigade, was killed by a Hezbollah explosive drone strike during operational activity in northern Israel. (Source: @almogboker78/17095)
Two IDF soldiers were wounded (moderate and light) by an explosive drone in southern Lebanon and evacuated to hospital for treatment; their families have been notified. (Source: @N12chat/208784)

Red alerts were activated in the Western Galilee (Rosh HaNikra, Batsat beach, Milouot North industrial zone, and Batsat) following suspected hostile aircraft intrusion; the event was later declared ended. (Source: @yediotnews25/129660)

A senior Israeli official criticized the emerging deal as bad because it signals to Iran that the Strait of Hormuz is a weapon as effective as a nuclear one, noting Trump views the deal as merely economic with mutual Strait opening. (Source: @amitsegal/61316)

Israeli officials are weighing strategic options for Lebanon: whether to be dragged into restraint in the style of October 6, engage in daily attrition warfare as currently conducted, or launch a comprehensive war to remove the Hezbollah threat. (Source: @amitsegal/61317)

IDF forces in the Gaza Strip conducted overnight strikes to destroy structures in multiple areas, with powerful explosions reported across southern Israel and the Shephelah region. (Source: @pkpoi/142153)

At least 15 explosive drones were launched at IDF forces in southern Lebanon and along the northern border over the past weekend, triggering 20 red alerts across multiple confrontation-line communities. (Source: @inon_yttach/8231)

IDF soldier Noam Hamburger was killed by a Hezbollah explosive drone strike at a base in northern Israel, marking a significant escalation in Hezbollah's drone campaign against Israeli military installations. (Source: @lelotsenzura/92114)
Two IDF soldiers were wounded (moderate and light) by an explosive drone in southern Lebanon and evacuated to hospital; their families have been notified. (Source: @N12chat/208784)

Israeli warplanes reportedly struck the town of Fron in southern Lebanon in two separate raids, indicating continued Israeli aerial operations against Hezbollah targets in the Nabatieh region. (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/360214)

Israeli sources report damage at the Rosh HaNikra site from a Hezbollah explosive drone strike, with footage showing the extent of destruction at the northern border location. (Source: @almogboker78/17086)

Intense jet activity was reported over eastern Iraq near Iran's western borders, suggesting continued coalition or Israeli aerial operations in the region targeting Iranian-backed militia infrastructure. (Source: @shin_persian/6169)

Reports from Saudi Arabia indicate a US-Iran agreement announcement is expected within hours, with Trump scheduled to speak with Netanyahu overnight; Republican Senator Lindsey Graham warned that if a deal is signed, it would shift the regional balance of forces and become a nightmare for Israel. (Source: @almogboker78/17098)

Trump held a video call with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed, Qatari Emir Tamim bin Hamad, Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif, Turkish President Erdogan, Egyptian President el-Sisi, Jordan's King Abdullah II, and Bahrain's King Hamad regarding Iran and the peace memorandum. (Source: @inon_yttach/8237)

Pakistan Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif expressed hope that Pakistan would soon host the next round of Tehran-Washington talks, positioning Islamabad as a potential mediator in the diplomatic process. (Source: @VahidOnline/75668)

N12 reported that Iran's Revolutionary Guards acquired Chinese-made surveillance and espionage equipment through a UAE-based trading network, with a Ras al-Khaimah communications company shipping approximately 1.8 tons of Chinese satellite antenna equipment from Shanghai to Iran via Dubai's Jebel Ali port. (Source: @N12chat/208788)

key_developments: US and Iran are reportedly close to a comprehensive peace agreement to end the 60-day war, with Axios indicating a deal could be announced within 24 hours, though disputes remain over key wording of several provisions. (Source: @kianmeli1/87611)

Hezbollah launched at least 15 explosive drones at IDF forces in southern Lebanon and along the northern border over the past weekend, killing one Israeli soldier and wounding two others, while triggering 20 red alerts in confrontation-line communities. (Source: @inon_yttach/8231)

The proposed US-Iran deal includes three phases: formal end of war, resolution of the Strait of Hormuz crisis, and 30-day negotiations on a broader agreement with possible extension, though Iran insists the Strait will remain under its management. (Source: @amitsegal/61308)

IRGC commander-in-chief issued a defiant statement declaring Iran's armed forces at highest readiness across all domains and warning that any renewed enemy aggression would receive a devastating hellish response across regional and extra-regional dimensions. (Source: @SharghDaily/161697)

Iranian intelligence dismantled a network of 15 suspects tied to two organized cells and a newly emerged terrorist group backed by foreign groups in Fars province before they could threaten public security. (Source: @presstv/191170)

Trump held a multilateral video call with leaders of Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan, and Bahrain regarding the Iran peace memorandum, while separately speaking with Netanyahu about the expected deal. (Source: @inon_yttach/8237)

Israeli officials are weighing three strategic options for Lebanon: restraint in the style of October 6, continued daily attrition warfare, or a comprehensive war to remove the Hezbollah threat, as Hezbollah's daily attacks continue unabated. (Source: @amitsegal/61317)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational environment remains highly volatile with simultaneous diplomatic progress and active combat operations. Hezbollah continues to pose an immediate and escalating threat to northern Israel through explosive drone attacks, having killed one IDF soldier and wounded others while launching at least 15 drones over a single weekend. Iran's IRGC has issued its most explicit threat to date, declaring readiness across all military domains and promising 'hellish' regional and extra-regional retaliation if attacked again. The potential peace deal introduces significant uncertainty: if signed, it could rapidly de-escalate the broader US-Iran conflict while leaving the Israel-Hezbollah front unresolved, potentially creating conditions for a separate northern war. The reported Chinese surveillance equipment pipeline through UAE ports to Iran's IRGC suggests ongoing Iranian efforts to enhance targeting capabilities even during negotiations.
sections.regional_response: Gulf states are actively engaging in the diplomatic process, with Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar, and other regional leaders participating in a Trump-led video call on the Iran peace memorandum. Pakistan has positioned itself as a potential mediator, with Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif offering to host future Tehran-Washington talks. Israel appears deeply concerned about the emerging deal, with senior officials warning it would shift the regional balance of forces and reward Iran's use of the Strait of Hormuz as a coercive weapon. Senator Lindsey Graham publicly warned that a deal would be a nightmare for Israel, questioning why the war was opened in the first place if the outcome is a negotiated settlement that preserves Iranian leverage.
sections.intelligence_notes: The reported Chinese-made espionage equipment shipment of approximately 1.8 tons of satellite antennas from Shanghai to Iran via Dubai's Jebel Ali port, facilitated by a Ras al-Khaimah-based company, indicates Iran's IRGC has been actively acquiring foreign surveillance technology during the conflict period. The contradiction between Iran's Fars news agency claiming the Strait of Hormuz will remain under Iranian management and Trump's assertion of 'free passage' suggests significant unresolved diplomatic gaps that could derail the agreement. The IRGC's explicit reference to 'extra-regional dimensions' in its threat statement may indicate planning for attacks on US bases in the broader Middle East or beyond. Israeli strategic deliberation over Lebanon policy — choosing between restraint, attrition, or comprehensive war — suggests the northern front will remain a critical flashpoint regardless of any US-Iran deal.
